title: Hive
aliases: Hive: A Game Crawling with Possibilities

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **22 hexagonal Bakelite tiles** (11 per player in black and white):
  - **1** Queen Bee
  - **2** Spiders
  - **2** Beetles
  - **3** Grasshoppers
  - **3** Soldier Ants
- **Expansion tiles** (optional): Mosquito, Ladybug, Pillbug (1 each per player)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
- **No board** — Tiles are placed directly on any flat surface.
- **The hive** forms organically as tiles are placed adjacent to each other.
- **First player** places any tile. Second player places a tile adjacent to it.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **11 tiles each** (or 14 with all expansions).
- **White goes first.**
- **Queen must be placed** by your 4th turn at the latest.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Hive is a boardless abstract strategy game. Place and move insect tiles to surround the opponent's Queen Bee on all 6 sides. No board is needed — tiles create the playing surface.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do ONE of:
1. **Place a new tile** from your supply adjacent to the hive.
2. **Move an existing tile** according to its movement rules.

If you can do neither, you must pass.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Placement Rules
- New tiles must be placed touching at least 1 tile of your own color.
- New tiles must NOT touch any opponent tiles (except on the very first two plays).
- **Queen by turn 4** — If your Queen isn't placed by your 4th turn, you must place it.
- You cannot move any of your tiles until your Queen is placed.

#### Piece Movement
- **Queen Bee** — Moves 1 space. Must stay connected to the hive.
- **Spider** — Moves exactly 3 spaces along the edge of the hive. Cannot backtrack.
- **Beetle** — Moves 1 space. Can climb on top of the hive, pinning the tile beneath.
- **Grasshopper** — Jumps in a straight line over adjacent tiles to the first empty space.
- **Soldier Ant** — Moves any number of spaces around the outside edge of the hive.

#### One Hive Rule
- The hive must always remain one connected group.
- A tile cannot be moved if it would split the hive into two separate groups.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Freedom to Move
- A tile can only slide into or out of a space if the gap is physically wide enough (no squeezing between two tiles).

#### Beetle Stacking
- Beetles can climb on top of any adjacent tile (friend or foe).
- A tile with a beetle on top cannot move.
- The beetle on top controls the color of that space for placement purposes.

#### Expansion Pieces
- **Mosquito** — Copies the movement of any adjacent piece.
- **Ladybug** — Moves exactly 3: 2 on top of the hive, then 1 down.
- **Pillbug** — Moves 1 space, OR moves an adjacent piece on top of itself and then to another adjacent space.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
- Completely surround your opponent's Queen Bee on all 6 sides (with any tiles, yours or theirs).

#### Draw
- If both Queens are simultaneously surrounded, the game is a draw.

#### No points — pure win/loss/draw.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Place Queen early** — Turn 2 or 3 is ideal; waiting until turn 4 limits your options.
- **Ants are powerful** — They can reach any spot on the hive edge; use them offensively.
- **Beetles for pinning** — Climb onto the opponent's Queen to immobilize it.
- **Don't isolate your Queen** — Keep your Queen near friendly pieces for defense.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Pin and surround** — Use Beetles to pin key pieces while Ants close the noose.
- **Grasshopper mobility** — Grasshoppers can jump across the hive; use them to reach distant positions.
- **One Hive defense** — Position your Queen so that pieces around it can't move without breaking the hive.
- **Tempo** — Each turn you place instead of move is one turn less pressure; balance offense and defense.
